episodes.title: University of Salford research - pitfalls to be aware of!
episodes.description:
We launched this survey back in May 2024. One or two pitfalls have been reported largely due to limitations of the survey platform. This video goes through then and we hope it is helpful.Below is the link to the survey itself and also to a Get a Life podcast in which we explain more about the research. 
The survey link:
https://app.onlinesurveys.jisc.ac.uk/s/salford/health-wellbeing-former-members
The Get a Life podcast:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=I30IoGJRbIA

episodes.title: Second and Multi-generation Adult Survivors of the Exclusive Brethren,
episodes.description: Second and Multi-generation Adult Survivors of the Exclusive Brethren,

episodes.title: When Endings are Beginnings: David
episodes.description: In this video and using extracts from David's blog we can learn something of the emotional and cognitive world of someone leaving a high demand group - in this case the Exclusive Brethren. Since the video was made, David got married to someone from a different country, they have a child and are doing really well.

episodes.title: Being raised in and leaving the Exclusive Brethren through the lens of attachment theory
episodes.description:
Paper given at the Division of Counselling Psychology Annual Conference in Newcastle 2018. 
With thanks to John Stocks for his editing assistance. 

Disclaimer - While I have striven to make the information in this paper as  accurate as possible, I make no claims, promises, or guarantees about the accuracy, completeness, or adequacy of the contents of this work, and expressly disclaim liability for errors and omissions in the contents of this paper. The paper contains my opinions and not hard facts.
